quotes.quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.
quotes.rationale: An internal selection mechanism gates which of 5,000 ideas advance to experimental validation, a stage transition.

quotes.quote: Specifically, it uses the classic Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) algorithm to select the most promising record.
quotes.rationale: A deterministic UCB rule controls which record is selected next, i.e. search control.

quotes.quote: This deeply validated record becomes a new record in the system's knowledge base, thus influencing the decision-making process in all subsequent cycles.
quotes.rationale: Validated records are admitted to memory and route back into subsequent decisions.

quotes.quote: The architecture of DeepScientist actualizes the Bayesian Optimization loop through a multi-agent system equipped with an open-knowledge system and a continuously accumulating Findings Memory. This memory is composed of both frontier human knowledge (e.g., papers and codes) and the system's own historical findings, and it intelligently guides subsequent explorations.
quotes.rationale: Findings Memory accumulates and guides subsequent exploration, closing the loop.

quotes.quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
quotes.rationale: Independent re-execution checks the coding agent's self-reported completion and quantifies a ~50% false-positive rate.

quotes.quote: Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.
quotes.rationale: In-loop human experts verify outputs and filter hallucinations.

quotes.quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
quotes.rationale: Human supervisors manually inspected all experimental results for authenticity.

quotes.quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.
quotes.rationale: DeepReviewer is employed by the authors post hoc to compare outputs against 28 papers; no routing back into the loop is reported.

quotes.quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.
quotes.rationale: A convened committee of three volunteer researchers renders a terminal assessment of the system's outputs.

quotes.quote: To make the AI a more capable partner, future work should focus on key enhancements: developing simulated discovery environments to accelerate learning via reinforcement, creating frameworks for integrating feedback from the scientific community, and ultimately, bridging the gap to the physical sciences through robotics.
quotes.rationale: Community-feedback integration and robotic physical validation are stated as future work, not exercised.

diagnostics.consequence: The most aggressive gate in the pipeline cannot be assessed for selectivity or bias, so the reported yield of 21 advances cannot be attributed to validation quality rather than filtering artifacts.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.

diagnostics.statement: The idea selection mechanism that reduces over 5,000 generated ideas to about 1,100 candidates for experimental validation is described only by its output counts; the packet does not report its substrate, criteria, or evidence source.

diagnostics.consequence: Any result path lacking the re-execution check or human inspection would admit roughly half-failed implementations as apparent successes into memory and downstream claims.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.

diagnostics.statement: The coding agent self-reports completion with roughly a 50% false-positive rate, requiring an independent re-execution step to detect the failures.

diagnostics.consequence: Quality claims rest on assessors selected and run by the proposers, with no agreement, discrimination, or bias study to bound their judgments.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.

diagnostics.statement: Both terminal assessment channels are commissioned by the proposing team: DeepReviewer is employed by the authors and the program committee is convened from volunteer researchers, with no reliability study reported for either.

diagnostics.consequence: The autonomy of the discovery loop is indeterminate, since an undocumented share of validation may be carried by humans rather than the automated channels.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.

A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.

diagnostics.statement: Human supervision is reported as a blanket verification and hallucination filter and as manual inspection of all results, but the criteria, decision rule, and override frequency are not stated.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.

recommendations.proposed_action: Document the idea selection mechanism's substrate, scoring criteria, and evidence inputs, and report retention and downstream success rates for selected versus rejected ideas rather than raw counts alone.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Selected ideas show a significantly higher progress rate than a random sample drawn from the rejected pool
success_check.evidence_needed: Ablation run in which sampled rejected ideas are executed and scored under the same pipeline
success_check.observable: Published specification of the selection mechanism plus per-stage retention and scientific-progress rates for selected versus rejected ideas

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report coverage and outcomes of the secondary re-execution check and the human inspection step, including how many candidate results each stage rejected, so the residual false-positive rate after verification is quantified.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Residual false-positive rate after verification reported with a bound substantially below the observed ~50% pre-verification rate
success_check.evidence_needed: Logged verification traces and supervisor decision records across all reported runs
success_check.observable: Stage-wise counts of results rejected by automated re-execution versus by human inspection, with documented rejection criteria

exact_quotes.exact_quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add a reliability study for the terminal review channels, such as blinded, provenance-masked scoring of system and control papers with agreement measured between the LLM reviewer and the human committee.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Agreement reported with confidence intervals and no systematic score advantage for system-authored papers once provenance is masked
success_check.evidence_needed: Blinded review protocol, per-paper scores from both channels, and control set composition
success_check.observable: Inter-rater agreement statistics between DeepReviewer and the human committee under blinding, plus scores on control papers of known provenance

memory_structure: continuously accumulating Findings Memory containing frontier human knowledge (papers and code) and the system's own historical findings
knowledge base of deeply validated records influencing decision-making in subsequent cycles
policy_type: Bayesian optimization loop over a multi-agent system
internal idea selection mechanism gating experimental validation
